pg90-p1-s1: When you discover a prospect for Alcoholics Anonymous, find out all you can about him.
pg90-p1-s2:
If he does not want to stop
drinking, don't waste time trying to persuade him.
pg90-p1-s3: You may spoil a later opportunity.
pg90-p1-s4:
This advice is given for his
family also.
pg90-p1-s5: They should be patient, realizing they are dealing with a sick person.
pg90-p1:
When you discover a prospect for Alcoholics Anonymous, find out all you can about him. If he does not want to stop
drinking, don't waste time trying to persuade him. You may spoil a later opportunity. This advice is given for his
family also. They should be patient, realizing they are dealing with a sick person.
pg90-p2-s1:
If there is any indication that he wants to stop, have a good talk with the person most interested in him - usually his
wife.
pg90-p2-s2:
Get an idea of his behavior, his problems, his background, the seriousness of his condition, and his religious
leanings.
pg90-p2-s3:
You need this information to put yourself in his place, to see how you would like him to approach you if the
tables were turned.
pg90-p2:
If there is any indication that he wants to stop, have a good talk with the person most interested in him - usually his
wife. Get an idea of his behavior, his problems, his background, the seriousness of his condition, and his religious
leanings. You need this information to put yourself in his place, to see how you would like him to approach you if the
tables were turned.
pg90-p3-s1: Sometimes it is wise to wait till he goes on a binge.
pg90-p3-s2:
The family may object to this, but unless he is in a dangerous
physical condition, it is better to risk it.
pg90-p3-s3:
Don't deal with him when he is very drunk, unless he is ugly and the family
needs your help.
pg90-p3-s4: Wait for the end of the spree, or at least for a lucid interval.
pg90-p3-s5:
Then let his family or a friend ask
him if he wants to quit for good and if he would go to any extreme to do so.
pg90-p3-s6:
If he says yes, then his attention should
be drawn to you as a person who has recovered.
pg90-p3-s7:
You should be described to him as one of a fellowship who, as part of
their own recovery, try to help others and who will be glad to talk to him if he cares to see you.
pg90-p3:
Sometimes it is wise to wait till he goes on a binge. The family may object to this, but unless he is in a dangerous
physical condition, it is better to risk it. Don't deal with him when he is very drunk, unless he is ugly and the family
needs your help. Wait for the end of the spree, or at least for a lucid interval. Then let his family or a friend ask
him if he wants to quit for good and if he would go to any extreme to do so. If he says yes, then his attention should
be drawn to you as a person who has recovered. You should be described to him as one of a fellowship who, as part of
their own recovery, try to help others and who will be glad to talk to him if he cares to see you.
pg90-p4-s1: If he does not want to see you, never force yourself upon him.
pg90-p4-s2:
Neither should the family hysterically plead with him to
do anything, nor should they tell him much about you.
pg90-p4-s3: They should wait for the end of his next drinking bout.
pg90-p4-s4:
You might
place this book where he can see it in the interval.
pg90-p4-s5: Here no specific rule can be given.
pg90-p4:
If he does not want to see you, never force yourself upon him. Neither should the family hysterically plead with him to
do anything, nor should they tell him much about you. They should wait for the end of his next drinking bout. You might
place this book where he can see it in the interval. Here no specific rule can be given. The family must decide these
